title: Explicit midpoint RK2 matches the quadratic Taylor polynomial on the scalar linear test equation

source.citation: Ernst Hairer, Syvert P. Norsett, and Gerhard Wanner, Solving Ordinary Differential Equations I: Nonstiff Problems, 2nd ed., Springer, 1993, Ch. II, pp. 129-353.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For the scalar linear test equation y prime equals a y, one explicit midpoint RK2 step equals the second-order Taylor polynomial (1 + dt * a + (dt * a)^2 / 2) times y.
informal.latex: For \(y\prime=a y\), midpoint RK2 gives \(y_+=(1+\Delta t\,a+(\Delta t\,a)^2/2)y\).
assumptions: dt, a, and y are real numbers
midpointRK2Step uses the explicit midpoint stage
linearTaylor2Step is the quadratic Taylor polynomial for the linear test equation
informal.expected_conclusion: The midpoint RK2 step equals the linear-test-equation quadratic Taylor step.

declarations.name: midpointRK2Step
declarations.statement: def midpointRK2Step (dt a y : Real) : Real := let k1 := a * y; let k2 := a * (y + (dt / 2) * k1); y + dt * k2

declarations.name: linearTaylor2Step
declarations.statement: def linearTaylor2Step (dt a y : Real) : Real := (1 + dt * a + (dt * a) ^ 2 / 2) * y

declarations.name: midpointRK2Step_eq_taylor2
declarations.statement: theorem midpointRK2Step_eq_taylor2 (dt a y : Real) : midpointRK2Step dt a y = linearTaylor2Step dt a y

tasks.input: Translate the explicit midpoint RK2 second-order Taylor identity for the scalar linear test equation into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem midpointRK2Step_eq_taylor2 (dt a y : Real) : midpointRK2Step dt a y = linearTaylor2Step dt a y

tasks.input: Given the midpointRK2Step and linearTaylor2Step definitions, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding midpointRK2Step and linearTaylor2Step, then using ring.
